behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at prompt/payload construction inside one module. A deterministic test can call the classifier with a fake client and assert the exact outbound payload includes all taxonomy labels. No OS/network/model behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate classifier with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and a capturing fake API client. Call classify(document). Assert captured request contains all labels (no omissions, no substitutions) in the field/prompt section used for classification.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic response-validation logic at a single boundary. A fake API response with an out-of-taxonomy label reproduces the failure directly; real I/O is unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fake API client that returns label="medical" while taxonomy excludes it. Assert classify(document) returns "unknown" and never returns "medical".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output stability. In-process mocks are typically deterministic and can hide true non-determinism from the external AI API. This must be validated against the real model behavior (or real production-equivalent model endpoint).
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real API classifications for the same fixed document under production settings (same model/options), e.g., N=20. Assert all returned labels are identical; fail on any drift. Record run-to-run variance to detect intermittent instability.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often appears at provider accounting boundaries (tokenization/overhead differences between local estimates and actual billed usage). Pure mocks can always pass and hide real over-budget calls. Requires real API usage measurement.
behaviors.test_description: With budget B configured, send representative documents via the real API path and capture provider-reported token usage per call. Assert usage <= B for each case, including request framing overhead used in production.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module retry/backoff behavior (API client + retry policy + timing). A unit test with trivial mocks can miss wiring and policy issues; full real network is not strictly required if retry semantics are exercised with realistic error objects and controllable time.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier with real retry/backoff components, fake clock/sleeper, and a stub transport/client that returns rate-limit error first then success. Assert: (1) classifier does not surface immediate error, (2) wait/backoff was invoked, (3) subsequent retry succeeds and returns label.
